content_hints.description: The helm of a pristine airship, brass fittings gleaming, steam gauge hissing steady at cruising pressure. A lone test pilot sits at the wheel, fingers white-knuckled on the grips. The moon is full and enormous through the forward glass, painting the clouds below in silver. Then the clouds part, and beneath them: not land, but a sea of glass -- the Glass Sea, where light bends wrong and compasses spin.
content_hints.read_aloud: The radio crackles: 'Lightkeeper Station to Test Flight Seven, you are approaching restricted airspace. The Glass Sea is a known navigational hazard. Advise immediate course correction.' The pilot does not respond. The steam gauge needle drops. Ahead, an inky mist rises from the glass surface like breath from a sleeping mouth. The airship shudders. Instruments spin. The mist opens, and inside it: wreckage. Dozens of hulls, interlocking like the teeth of a maw. The airship plunges in.
event_triggers: A test pilot's airship crashes into a mass of interlocking shipwrecks suspended above the Glass Sea. The wreckage has been accumulating for at least a decade. No survivors have been recovered from any prior disappearance.
The Glass Sea: a region where bleed concentrations warp light, gravity, and radio transmissions. Known for unexplained disappearances. EONS has been monitoring it for years.

content_hints.description: The wreckage. The airship's envelope has torn on a spar of twisted metal, spilling hydrogen into the mist. The gondola hangs at a thirty-degree angle, lodged in the ribcage of a much older vessel. Through the shattered forward glass, you can see the scope of the graveyard: ships from every era -- sailing vessels, steamers, early airships -- all crushed and woven together on a platform of dark stone that should not exist this high above the sea.
content_hints.read_aloud: The airship's envelope has torn on a spar of twisted metal, hydrogen hissing into the mist. The gondola hangs at a sickening angle, lodged in the ribcage of a much older vessel. Through the shattered glass, the scope of the graveyard reveals itself -- ships from every era, sailing vessels and steamers and early airships, all crushed and woven together on a platform of dark stone that should not exist.
event_triggers: The pilot's mayday was received by Lightkeeper Station. Candela Obscura is called in. The bleed readings from the Glass Sea have spiked to unprecedented levels.
content_hints.perception_success: The stone platform beneath the wreckage is not natural. It has been carved -- or grown. Its surface is covered in the same geometric patterns found on ancient bleed containment vessels. Something was built here, long before the first ship disappeared.
